articles.title: The White House Is Making Up Its Rules for AI in Real Time
articles.subtitle: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.
articles.author: Maxwell Zeff
articles.summary: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.
body: It’s been nearly a week since the Trump administration sent an export control directive to Anthropic, forcing one of the world’s leading AI labs to pull its most advanced models offline. After days of negotiations between Anthropic and the White House, the two still remain at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Why? Well, it depends whom you ask.

Throughout this entire debacle, Anthropic doesn’t believe it violated any concrete procedures or rules laid out by the Trump administration, according to a person close to the company. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly, demonstrating that it can’t be trusted to safely roll out frontier technology.

This saga proved to me that we’re now officially in the Wild West era of American AI regulation. While there are few laws on the books governing frontier AI development, that doesn’t mean companies won’t end up in trouble with Trump’s White House when they cross unspoken lines.

“The problem here is that the White House has been in this extreme anti-regulatory posture, and they’re now faced with the real AI capabilities that people have been predicting for many years,” says a former White House technology official, who asked to remain anonymous to avoid jeopardizing their professional relationships. “There should have been preparation and policies to systematically deal with this, managing the benefits and risks, but instead it's just this slap-dash approach that puts the AI industry in a real quandary.”

The Trump administration has repeatedly blocked efforts to impose guardrails on the AI industry, often arguing the rules could hamper US innovation and lead the country to fall behind rivals like China. Since he returned to the White House, President Trump has signed executive orders that reversed a Biden-era effort to create a national AI framework and created a federal task force to challenge state laws that could be deemed onerous .

While WIRED and other publications have made details of the negotiations between Anthropic and the White House public over the last week, the dispute is still defined by its opaqueness. At no point has the US government clearly stated what Anthropic did wrong—the best we have is a post on X outlining the general situation from White House technology adviser David Sacks.

Ironically, the White House’s actions have likely hampered the very kind of innovation it wants to protect. The Trump administration demanded that Anthropic prohibit all foreign nationals from accessing Mythos and Fable 5, preventing many of the AI lab’s own employees from accessing its most cutting-edge models, which the company says have sped up its research and development in recent months. All of Anthropic’s customers are locked out, too, including Apple, Meta, and much of the Fortune 500.

The White House may have had legitimate reasons to be concerned about Anthropic’s models. As my colleagues and I reported on Wednesday, US officials grew concerned when they learned earlier this month that Anthropic shared Mythos with SK Telecom , a South Korean telecom giant they allege has ties to China. Separately, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy raised concerns to US Treasury secretary Scott Bessent that some of the guardrails on Claude Fable 5, a safeguarded version of Mythos, could be circumvented.

Even if these worries are legitimate, that doesn’t mean the White House handled them adequately. In the first case, Anthropic says it coordinated with the US government on the rollout of Mythos, suggesting there may have been a chance for officials to raise alarm about SK Telecom ahead of time. Anthropic has also been working with the Korean company for years, and the arrangement seemingly never caused it to run into national security problems before. And when the White House raised concerns about SK Telecom to Anthropic, it revoked access to the model immediately, we reported.

In the second case, WIRED and other outlets have written at length about how every large language model can be jailbroken to varying extents. Anthropic and independent cybersecurity researchers argue that solving jailbreaks is not an easy or isolated issue. Because AI models are probabilistic, not deterministic, tech companies can’t guarantee what exactly they will generate in response to a given prompt. If the White House won’t let Anthropic release Claude Fable 5 until the jailbreaking problem is solved, I wouldn’t hold my breath.

But ultimately, the problem here is not that the US government is trying to ensure that advanced AI models have proper safeguards or stay out of the hands of America’s foes. It’s that the Trump administration is now in a position where it has to make these regulatory decisions in real time.

I can assure you that other AI labs like OpenAI, Google, and Meta have been watching Anthropic’s misfortune with bated breath. I’ve been asking AI executives the same question all week: How can you avoid a fate similar to Anthropic’s?
keyPoints: It’s been nearly a week since the Trump administration sent an export control directive to Anthropic, forcing one of the world’s leading AI labs to pull its most advanced models offline. After days of negotiations between Anthropic and the White House, the two still remain at odds about how to bring Claude Mythos and Fable 5 back. Why? Well, it depends whom you ask.

Throughout this entire debacle, Anthropic doesn’t believe it violated any concrete procedures or rules laid out by the Trump administration, according to a person close to the company. But the White House contends that Anthropic behaved recklessly, demonstrating that it can’t be trusted to safely roll out frontier technology.

This saga proved to me that we’re now officially in the Wild West era of American AI regulation. While there are few laws on the books governing frontier AI development, that doesn’t mean companies won’t end up in trouble with Trump’s White House when they cross unspoken lines.
paragraphTranslations: 距离特朗普政府向人类补全公司发送出口控制指令，迫使这家世界领先的人工智能实验室之一将其最先进的模型下线已经快一周了。在人类补全公司和白宫之间经过数天的谈判后，双方仍然对如何恢复克劳德·米索斯和法布尔5持不同意见。为什么呢？这取决于你问谁。
在整个这场困境中，人类补全公司不认为自己违反了特朗普政府制定的任何具体程序或规则，据一位接近公司的人说。但白宫坚称人类补全公司的行为鲁莽，表明它不能被信任安全推出前沿技术。
这段经历让我确信，我们现在正式进入了美国人工智能监管的荒野时代。虽然法律书籍中很少有关于前沿人工智能开发的法律，但这并不意味着公司在越过未言明的界限时不会遇到特朗普白宫的麻烦。
“这里的问题是，白宫一直采取这种极端的反监管立场，现在他们面临着人们多年来一直预测的真正的人工智能能力，”一位要求匿名以避免危及其职业关系的前白宫技术官员说。“应该有准备和政策来系统地应对这一问题，管理利益和风险，但相反，这只是这种临时拼凑的方法，让人工智能行业陷入真正的困境。”
特朗普政府一再阻止对人工智能行业施加监管的努力，经常争辩说规则可能会阻碍美国的创新，导致国家落后于中国等竞争对手。自从他回到白宫以来，特朗普总统签署了行政命令，扭转了拜登时代创建国家人工智能框架的努力，并创建了一个联邦特别工作组，挑战可能被视为繁琐的国家法律。
尽管《连线》杂志和其他出版物在过去一周公开了人类补全公司和白宫之间的谈判细节，但争端仍然以其不透明性为特点。美国政府从未明确声明人类补全公司做错了什么——我们所拥有的最好的东西是白宫技术顾问大卫·萨克斯在X上发表的一篇概述一般情况的帖子。
具有讽刺意味的是，白宫的行动可能阻碍了它想要保护的那种创新。特朗普政府要求人类补全公司禁止所有外国公民访问米索斯和法布尔5，阻止了人工智能实验室自己的许多员工访问其最先进的模型，公司表示这些模型加快了其近几个月的研究和开发。人类补全公司的所有客户也被锁定在外，包括苹果、Meta和财富500强的大部分。
白宫可能有合理的理由对人类补全公司的模型表示担忧。正如我和我的同事们在周三报道的那样，美国官员在本月早些时候得知人类补全公司与SK电讯共享米索斯时感到担忧，SK电讯是他们声称与中国有联系的韩国电信巨头。另外，亚马逊首席执行官安迪·贾西向美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特提出了一些关于克劳德·法布尔5的监管措施可能被绕过的担忧，克劳德·法布尔5是米索斯的一个受保护版本。
即使这些担忧是合理的，那并不意味着白宫处理得当。在第一种情况下，人类补全公司表示它与美国政府协调推出了米索斯，暗示可能有机会让官员提前对SK电讯提出警报。人类补全公司也与韩国公司合作多年，这种安排似乎从未导致它遇到国家安全问题。当白宫向人类补全公司提出对SK电讯的担忧时，它立即撤销了对模型的访问，我们报道了这一点。
在第二种情况下，《连线》和其他媒体已经详细报道了每个大型语言模型都可以在不同程度上被越狱。人类补全公司和独立的网络安全研究人员认为，解决越狱问题不是一个容易或孤立的问题。因为人工智能模型是概率性的，不是确定性的，科技公司不能保证它们对给定提示的确切反应。如果白宫不让人类补全公司发布克劳德·法布尔5，直到越狱问题得到解决，我不会屏息以待。
但归根结底，问题不在于美国政府试图确保先进的人工智能模型有适当的安全措施，或者不让它们落入美国的敌人手中。而在于特朗普政府现在处于一个必须实时做出这些监管决策的位置。
我可以向你保证，其他人工智能实验室如OpenAI、谷歌和Meta一直在屏息关注人类补全公司的不幸。我一直在问人工智能高管同一个问题：你如何避免与人类补全公司类似的命运？

vocabulary.example: The Trump administration made new rules.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 特朗普政府制定了新规则。

vocabulary.example: AI is changing the world.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 人工智能正在改变世界。

vocabulary.example: Export control is important for national security.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 出口控制对国家安全很重要。

vocabulary.example: The new regulation affects many businesses.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 新法规影响了许多企业。

vocabulary.example: Innovation is key to success.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 创新是成功的关键。

paragraphs: The Trump administration recently forced Anthropic, a leading AI lab, to take down its advanced AI models, Claude Mythos and Fable 5, due to export control issues. Anthropic claims it followed the rules, while the White House accuses it of reckless behavior. This incident highlights the lack of clear AI regulation in the US, with the Trump administration opposing regulations that might hinder innovation. The dispute is still unclear, as the US government has not specified Anthropic's wrongdoings. The White House's actions may have inadvertently stifled innovation, as Anthropic's models were used by many, including Apple and Meta. US officials were also concerned about Anthropic sharing Mythos with SK Telecom, a South Korean company with alleged ties to China.
paragraphTranslations: 特朗普政府最近迫使领先的人工智能实验室Anthropic下线其先进的人工智能模型Claude Mythos和Fable 5，因为出口控制问题。Anthropic声称它遵循了规则，而白宫则指责其行为鲁莽。这一事件突显了美国缺乏明确的人工智能法规，特朗普政府反对可能阻碍创新的法规。这场争议仍然不明朗，因为美国政府尚未具体说明Anthropic的不当行为。白宫的行动可能无意中抑制了创新，因为许多公司，包括苹果和Meta，都在使用Anthropic的模型。美国官员还对Anthropic与SK Telecom分享Mythos表示担忧，SK Telecom是一家被指控与中国有联系的韩国公司。

articles.title: Microplastics in over 75% of pet food, study finds
articles.subtitle: Researchers say certain brands and kinds of cat and dog food have more microplastics than others.
articles.author: BBC Science
articles.summary: Researchers say certain brands and kinds of cat and dog food have more microplastics than others.
body: Microplastics are in more than three-quarters of pet food products, a new study has found.
The research by the universities of Sussex and Exeter found microplastics in 16 out of the 19 brands that were tested, including "very well-known ones".
Cheaper "value range" products given to dogs, cats and wild animals contained more than the expensive ones, it found.
Microplastics are tiny plastic particles, typically smaller than 5mm.
Several studies have suggested that microplastics can harm animals and humans in many ways, though working out their exact impact is tricky.
Researchers in Sussex and Exeter said that the presence of plastic pollution in pet food may also have potential implications for health, but this was not the focus of their study.
The study found that, despite higher concentrations in dry food, wet food leads to higher daily microplastic intake because pets need to eat more of it to meet their energy requirements.

Researchers are urging government to put in new regulations to insist processed food manufacturers test for microplastic contamination - similar to the legislation that already exists to prevent chemical contamination.
"Our results are a reminder that our pets are exposed to the same chemical pollutants as ourselves," University of Exeter Prof of Ecotoxicology Tamara Galloway said.

"Pets may be inadvertently spreading plastic pollution through their food and faeces, affecting wildlife and the wider environment," Fiona Mathews, Prof of Environmental Biology at the University of Sussex, added.
Researchers said that the sources of contamination were unclear.
"Further research is now needed to find out whether ingredient quality, packaging types, or processing methods, are the main contributors to the problem," they added.
keyPoints: Microplastics are in more than three-quarters of pet food products, a new study has found.
The research by the universities of Sussex and Exeter found microplastics in 16 out of the 19 brands that were tested, including "very well-known ones".
Cheaper "value range" products given to dogs, cats and wild animals contained more than the expensive ones, it found.
paragraphTranslations: 一项新研究发现，超过四分之三的宠物食品中都含有微塑料。
苏塞克斯大学和埃克塞特大学的研究在测试的19个品牌中有16个品牌发现了微塑料，包括“非常知名”的品牌。
研究发现，给狗、猫和野生动物的更便宜的“价值范围”产品中微塑料含量超过了昂贵的产品。
微塑料是通常小于5毫米的微小塑料颗粒。
一些研究表明，微塑料可能以多种方式危害动物和人类，尽管确定它们的确切影响是困难的。
苏塞克斯和埃克塞特的研究人员表示，宠物食品中塑料污染的存在可能也对健康有潜在影响，但这不是他们研究的重点。
研究发现，尽管干食品中微塑料浓度更高，但湿食品导致每天微塑料摄入量更高，因为宠物需要吃更多的湿食品来满足它们的能量需求。
研究人员敦促政府制定新法规，要求加工食品制造商检测微塑料污染——类似于已经存在的防止化学污染的立法。
“我们的结果提醒我们，我们的宠物和我们自己一样暴露在相同的化学污染物中，”埃克塞特大学生态毒理学教授塔玛拉·加洛韦说。
“宠物可能通过它们的食物和粪便无意中传播塑料污染，影响野生动物和更广泛的环境，”苏塞克斯大学环境生物学教授菲奥娜·马修斯补充道。
研究人员表示，污染的来源尚不清楚。
“现在需要进一步的研究来找出是原料质量、包装类型还是加工方法，是问题的主要贡献者，”他们补充道。

vocabulary.example: Microplastics are a major environmental concern.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 微塑料是主要的环境问题。

vocabulary.example: High concentrations of chemicals can be dangerous.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 高浓度的化学物质可能是危险的。

vocabulary.example: A balanced diet is important for a healthy nutrient intake.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 均衡饮食对健康的营养摄入很重要。

vocabulary.example: New regulations were introduced to protect the environment.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 引入了新的法规来保护环境。

vocabulary.example: Water contamination is a serious issue.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 水污染是一个严重的问题。

vocabulary.example: The spread of disease can be linked to contaminated faeces.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 疾病传播可能与受污染的粪便有关。

paragraphs: A recent study by the universities of Sussex and Exeter found that microplastics are present in over 75% of pet food products. Out of 19 brands tested, 16 contained microplastics, including well-known ones. The study discovered that cheaper pet food had higher concentrations of microplastics than more expensive brands. Microplastics are small plastic particles, usually smaller than 5mm, and their impact on health is still not fully understood. Wet food was found to contribute more to daily microplastic intake due to pets needing more of it for energy. Researchers are calling for new regulations to test for microplastic contamination in pet food, similar to those for chemical contamination.
paragraphTranslations: 最近，苏塞克斯大学和埃克塞特大学的一项研究发现，超过75%的宠物食品中存在微塑料。在测试的19个品牌中，有16个含有微塑料，包括一些众所周知的品牌。研究发现，便宜的宠物食品中微塑料的浓度比昂贵的品牌更高。微塑料是小塑料颗粒，通常小于5毫米，它们对健康的影响尚未完全了解。由于宠物需要更多的湿粮来满足能量需求，湿粮被发现对日常微塑料摄入量贡献更大。研究人员呼吁制定新的法规，对宠物食品中的微塑料污染进行测试，类似于化学污染的法规。
